title: crepis trichocephala
intro: Crepis trichocephala, also known as the Rough Hawk's-beard or Rough Hawksbeard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Western Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. Crepis trichocephala is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as dried and powdered for use in capsules or tinctures.
